summary: Unit tests thoroughly cover the core checker.ts module (parsing, classifying, reporting), but critically omit CLI testing (src/cli.ts). The test harness uses act for end-to-end validation, but no unit tests exist for argument parsing, file I/O, or exit codes—the actual CLI interface. Edge cases are under-tested: malformed requirements.txt entries, unusual version formats, empty config lists, and lookup error conditions. Mock license lookup is functional but doesn't test failure paths. Test organization is clean with good naming, but the absence of CLI unit tests means regressions in the entry point would not be caught by `bun test`. With end-to-end act testing as a safety net, moderate confidence in core logic; low confidence in CLI robustness.
